free_text: Measurement of the cross section for the
neutron-induced fission of 238U nuclei in the energy
Neutron complex GNEIS on the basis of
~10 nsec, repetition rate up to 50 Hz.
Layers uniformity was measured by alpha-activity using
Si semiconductor detectors and was found to be 10%.
To ensure identical conditions for measurements of
fission cross sections, a 0.1-mm-thick Al screen was
placed on the active layer of the U-238 and U-235
targets and was used to separate a circular region
with the same diameter of ~48.0 mm on the surface of
on Al backing of 0.1 mm thickness.
Layer thickness 203+/-11 ug/cm2, size 50 mm x 100 mm,
on Al backing of 0.1 mm thickness.
The total registration efficiency of the detector
(assembly of two MWPCs) of ~45% is determined by the
solid angle of registration and the transparency of
3 anodes and 2 cathodes through which the fragment
must fly before it is registered.
Request of data was sent 10.07.2023 to A.S.Vorobyev.
Data were received from A.Vorobyev and
Data of REL-REF(41608) were revised and included in
analysis together with new measurement data.
Data of other 10 experiments, used for comparison.
Uncertainty due to angular resolution and
Authors' results are compared with 4 data sets.
Authors' results are compared with 8 data sets.
- anisotropy of fission fragments and limited solid
- isotopic content (<1% at En>1 MeV, maximum 8% at
238U/235U and 238U datasets at 1-500 MeV on Figs.5
Uncertainties in Table 1 of JETP Lett.117(2023)557.
Of authors. U-235 standard uncertainty is not included
in error bars on Fig.6 of JETP Lett.117(2023)557.
